source_quote: Board completely unresponsive after being left plugged-in while off — no LCD, no LPC wakeup indication, nothing happens when the power key is pressed. Likely cause: LPC system controller enters an unrecoverable state; a firmware bug prevents it from leaving its locked-up condition. Try the LPC reset button (nearest the battery connector); if that fails, the only reliable recovery is disconnecting and reconnecting the battery cables.

evidence.reasoning: The quote mentions 'LPC system controller' (alias of canonical 'LPC controller'); the bridge maps LPC controller to refdes U14.
Complete unresponsiveness with no LPC indication matches a 'dead' LPC mode.

evidence.source_quote_substring: no LCD, no LPC wakeup indication
evidence.reasoning: U14 sources rail LPC_VCC per the graph; the symptom of no LPC wakeup is consistent with LPC_VCC being offline.

source_quote: Board won't turn on; D9 LED is dark when power supply is connected (both 24 V barrel and USB-C PD 20 V). Likely cause: LPC firmware or power-path fault preventing the system from initialising; often triggered after deep-discharge or battery mishandling events. It stopped working: it was not turning on, and when the power supply was connected (both USB-C PD 20 V and the enclosed 24 V adapter) the D9 LED was dark.

evidence.reasoning: Quote explicitly names D9 LED; bridge maps canonical D9 to refdes D9.
A dark indicator LED with valid supply is consistent with the LED itself being dead (anti-pattern, local-only).

source_quote: Kernel log flooded with `reform2_lpc spi1.0: lpc_command: checksum mismatch` errors; battery status unavailable. Likely cause: LPC SPI communication broken — often correlated with a concurrent USB port malfunction, possibly suggesting a power or ground integrity problem on the SPI bus. One USB port was not functioning properly; subsequently the kernel log filled at high speed with `reform2_lpc spi1.0: lpc_command: c/0 checksum mismatch: af expected, 0 received`.

evidence.reasoning: Quote mentions LPC (alias of LPC controller); bridge maps to U14.
Marginal power integrity on LPC supply modelled as regulating_low.
Power integrity problem suggests sagging rail; 85% nominal is a typical marginal value.

source_quote: Charging board (battery/charge board) completely dead; machine will not charge or power on from batteries. Likely cause: Hardware failure on the separate charge board PCB. When the charge board is dead, other community posts describe connecting pins 2 and 3 on the J1 plug to bypass it temporarily and allow firmware reflashing while a replacement board is sourced.

evidence.reasoning: Quote names 'J1 plug' (alias of J1 connector); bridge maps to refdes J1.

evidence.source_quote_substring: machine will not charge or power on from batteries
evidence.reasoning: An open J1 connector breaks battery connection to the motherboard.
